title: feat(enter): scroll-area primitive, fix mobile drawer footer
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
Introduces a `<ScrollArea>` primitive that bundles the auto-hide scrollbar behavior with a themed thumb color, and uses it to fix a layout bug in the mobile drawer where the sign-in / account section could slide below the fold on short viewports.

- New `<ScrollArea theme? axis?>` in `frontend/src/components/ui/`. Thumb color comes from `--theme-scrollbar-thumb`, defined per `[data-theme="…"]` block in `style.css` — same cascade as `bg-theme-*`. Replaces the seven standalone `.scrollbar-theme-*` classes.
- Migrated all scrollers to the primitive: dashboard rail nav, main content, multi-select dropdown, both key dialogs, auth modal, design showcase. Deleted the now-redundant `use-auto-hide-scrollbar` hook.
- Mobile drawer fix: logo (top) and account area (bottom) are pinned; nav, API Reference, and legal links scroll between. Previously the entire rail scrolled as one block, so on a short viewport the Sign in button could be pushed below the visible area.
- Dropped the unused `gray` variant from `DashboardTheme` and the `asThemeName` shim it forced in `dashboard-section.tsx`.
- Added a `<ScrollAreaDemo>` section to `/internal/design`.

gist.headline: The scrollbar glow-up
gist.blurb: We rebuilt our scrollbars to be theme-aware and auto-hiding. More importantly, the mobile menu now pins the sign-in button to the bottom so it never gets lost off-screen.
gist.summary: Introduces a new `<ScrollArea>` primitive that replaces seven standalone CSS classes and fixes the mobile drawer by pinning the logo and account areas while the navigation scrolls.
gist.impact: Mobile users will always see the sign-in button regardless of screen height, and scrollbars across the dashboard now automatically hide and match the active theme.
gist.visual_concept: Polly the bee acting as a mechanic, adjusting a large scrollbar on a retro mobile phone screen. The screen shows a pinned top and bottom section with a scrolling middle. Cozy, warm lighting.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly the bee mascot wearing a tiny tool belt, adjusting a glowing lime green (#ecf874) scrollbar on a giant retro mobile phone screen. The phone displays a chunky UI with pinned top and bottom sections. Soft pastel gradient background with warm lighting, lo-fi 8-bit aesthetic like Stardew Valley. Nostalgic but beautiful, emotionally warm.
